'''Otoh Jahai''' was a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[City/Legends|city]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in [[3000 BBY]]. It was the site of an ancient [[Gungan Temple]].

==Description==
Otoh Jahai was a [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The city's buildings included a [[Barracks/Legends|troop center]], a [[command center]], a [[Gungan Temple]], [[prefab shelter]]s, and [[Sentry tower/Legends|sentry towers]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
During a [[War of the Gungan tribes|war]] between the various Gungan tribes in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[3000 BBY]],<ref name="EA" /> Otoh Jahai was under the leadership of [[Boss/Legends|Boss]] [[Tenko]]. The city participated in the fighting, and its troops targeted the nearby city of [[Otoh Langua/Legends|Otoh Langua]], ruled by Boss [[Hantic]], when the [[Sacred Staff]] of Otoh Jahai came up missing. When Boss [[Gallo/Legends|Gallo]] of [[Otoh Sancture]] visited the settlement to request Tenko's aid in his fight against [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] Boss [[Rogoe/Legends|Rogoe]], Tenko agreed to a ceasefire with Hantic only if Gallo retrieved and returned their stolen staff. Gallo set out to find the staff and discovered that [[Rogoe's army|Rogoe's forces]] had taken it rather than the people of Otoh Langua. Gallo returned the staff to Tenko, and in return Tenko donated four heavy [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] to Gallo's recently formed [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]]. Gallo went on to defeat Rogoe during a [[Raid on Spearhead|battle]] in Rogoe's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city of [[Spearhead/Legends|Spearhead]], and the Gungan tribes were united.<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Otoh Jahai appears in the Gungan campaign in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' and its [[2002]] expansion pack [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']].
